results.email: Hi, I was charged $49.99 twice this month for my Pro subscription. Can you refund the duplicate charge? Order confirmation numbers are #4471 and #4472, both dated the 3rd.
results.expected_summary: Customer reports a duplicate subscription charge and requests a refund.
results.actual_summary: Customer was charged twice ($49.99 each) for their Pro subscription on the 3rd and is requesting a refund for the duplicate charge (order #4471 and #4472).
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core issue (duplicate subscription charge and refund request) as the reference, with additional accurate details from the email that don't contradict anything in the reference.

results.email: why does my invoice say net 30 but the portal shows overdue after 15 days??? this is the second time this has happened
results.expected_summary: Customer flags a discrepancy between stated and enforced invoice payment terms.
results.actual_summary: Customer's invoice states Net 30 payment terms, but the portal is incorrectly marking it as overdue after only 15 days — this is a recurring issue.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core issue — a discrepancy between stated invoice payment terms (Net 30) and enforced terms (overdue after 15 days) — and also correctly notes it is a recurring issue, which aligns fully with the reference summary's meaning.

results.email: Every time I try to export my report as a PDF the app just freezes on 'Generating...' and I have to force-quit. Happens on both Chrome and Safari, macOS 14.5.
results.expected_summary: Customer's PDF export feature freezes the app across multiple browsers.
results.actual_summary: Customer is experiencing an app freeze during PDF export of reports, occurring on both Chrome and Safari browsers on macOS 14.5.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core issue (app freezing during PDF export) across multiple browsers (Chrome and Safari), with the additional detail of macOS 14.5 which doesn't contradict or misrepresent the reference.

results.expected_summary: Customer reports the app is not working, with no further detail provided.
results.actual_summary: Customer is reporting that the app is broken.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary conveys the same meaning as the reference — the customer reports the app is not working/broken — with no missing or incorrect information.

results.email: I reset my password twice using the email link but it still says incorrect password when I try to log in. Locked out for two days now.
results.expected_summary: Customer remains locked out of their account despite resetting their password twice.
results.actual_summary: Customer has been locked out of their account for two days despite resetting their password twice via email link, as the system still shows incorrect password.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures all the same core information as the reference (locked out despite resetting password twice) and adds additional details from the original email (two days, via email link, incorrect password error) without contradicting anything.

results.email: can u chnage the email on my acount from old@gmial.com to new@gmail.com thanksss
results.expected_summary: Customer requests to change the email address associated with their account.
results.actual_summary: Customer is requesting to update their account email address from old@gmial.com to new@gmail.com.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core request as the reference (changing the account email address) and additionally includes the specific old and new email addresses, which is consistent with the original email and does not contradict the reference.

results.email: Love the new dashboard redesign, way easier to find what I need now. Any plans to add dark mode?
results.expected_summary: Customer gives positive feedback on the dashboard redesign and asks about dark mode.
results.actual_summary: Customer compliments the new dashboard redesign and inquires about plans to add a dark mode feature.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core information as the reference: positive feedback on the dashboard redesign and an inquiry about dark mode, just using slightly different but equivalent phrasing.

results.email: Oh great, ANOTHER update that changes where the export button is. Really appreciate having to relearn your UI every month. Fantastic work as always.
results.expected_summary: Customer sarcastically criticizes the frequency of UI changes.
results.actual_summary: Customer is frustrated that a recent UI update moved the location of the export button, requiring them to relearn the interface.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate captures the core frustration and specific detail about the export button moving, but misses the reference's emphasis on the *frequency* of UI changes (sarcastic "every month") which is the main point of the complaint, not just a single instance.

results.email: Hola, mi factura de este mes es incorrecta, me cobraron por 3 usuarios pero solo tenemos 2 licencias activas.
results.expected_summary: Customer says this month's invoice incorrectly charged for 3 users instead of 2.
results.actual_summary: The customer reports an incorrect charge on their monthly invoice, as they were billed for 3 users but only have 2 active licenses.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures exactly the same information as the reference: an incorrect monthly invoice charge for 3 users when only 2 are active, just with slightly different but equivalent phrasing.

results.email: API returning 500 errors intermittently on POST /v2/orders since ~14:00 UTC today. About 1 in 5 requests. Retrying doesn't help. Need urgent help, this is affecting our checkout flow in production.
results.expected_summary: Customer reports intermittent 500 errors on a production API endpoint affecting checkout.
results.actual_summary: Customer is experiencing intermittent 500 errors (~1 in 5 requests) on POST /v2/orders since 14:00 UTC, impacting their production checkout flow, and retries are not resolving the issue.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures all the core information in the reference (intermittent 500 errors, production API endpoint, affecting checkout) and adds additional accurate details from the original email without contradicting anything.

results.email: This is the third email I'm sending. I need my account deleted and all my data removed per GDPR. Please confirm within 30 days as required by law.
results.expected_summary: Customer requests account deletion and data removal under GDPR, noting repeated prior contact.
results.actual_summary: Customer is requesting account deletion and full data removal in compliance with GDPR, and demands confirmation within the legally required 30-day window.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate captures the core GDPR account deletion and data removal request accurately, but omits the minor detail about repeated prior contact (third email), which is present in the reference summary.

results.email: Do you offer volume discounts for teams over 200 seats? Would like to talk to sales.
results.expected_summary: Prospective customer asks about volume discounts and requests to speak with sales.
results.actual_summary: Customer is inquiring about volume discounts for teams over 200 seats and would like to be connected with sales.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core information as the reference: the customer is asking about volume discounts and wants to speak with sales, with the additional detail about "200 seats" which is consistent with the original email and doesn't contradict the reference.

results.email: not sure if this is a bug or if im just dumb but the search bar doesnt find anything even when i type the exact title of a document i KNOW exists
results.expected_summary: Customer reports that search fails to find documents even with exact title matches.
results.actual_summary: The customer is reporting that the search bar is not returning any results, even when searching for the exact title of a known existing document.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core issue as the reference: search fails to return results even when using exact document titles, with no missing or incorrect information.

results.email: downgrade me to the free plan effective immediately, and I want a prorated refund for the unused days on my current plan
results.expected_summary: Customer requests an immediate downgrade to the free plan and a prorated refund.
results.actual_summary: Customer requests an immediate downgrade to the free plan and a prorated refund for the unused days remaining on their current paid plan.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures all the same core information as the reference (immediate downgrade to free plan and prorated refund), with the additional detail about "unused days remaining on their current paid plan" which is directly from the original email and doesn't contradict anything.

results.expected_summary: Customer's message consists only of '2fa', with no further context provided.
results.actual_summary: The customer is inquiring about or having an issue with two-factor authentication (2FA) on their account.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary infers a specific intent (inquiring about or having an issue with 2FA) that is not present in the original email; the reference correctly notes that the message consists only of '2fa' with no further context, meaning no such interpretation can be made.

results.email: Hey! Are you guys hiring? I'm a huge fan of the product and would love to work there.
results.expected_summary: Customer expresses interest in job opportunities at the company.
results.actual_summary: The customer is inquiring about job opportunities at the company, expressing interest in working there as a fan of the product.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core information as the reference — the customer is interested in job opportunities — and additionally includes the detail about being a fan of the product, which is accurate and does not contradict anything in the reference.

results.email: getting a weird error, screenshot attached: 'Error 0x8007045D: cannot sync'. tried restarting, tried reinstalling, tried a different network. nothing works. this started right after your update yesterday.
results.expected_summary: Customer reports a persistent sync error that began after a recent update, despite multiple troubleshooting attempts.
results.actual_summary: Customer is experiencing a persistent sync error (Error 0x8007045D) that started after a recent update, and has already attempted restarting, reinstalling, and switching networks without success.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures all the same core information as the reference — persistent sync error, triggered by a recent update, and multiple failed troubleshooting attempts — while additionally including the specific error code and the exact troubleshooting steps, which are accurate details from the email.

results.email: I cancelled my subscription last week but I can still see I'm being billed. Also separately, is there a way to get an itemized receipt for tax purposes?
results.expected_summary: Customer reports being billed after cancellation and asks about obtaining an itemized receipt.
results.actual_summary: Customer is being billed after cancelling their subscription and is also requesting an itemized receipt for tax purposes.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures both core issues from the reference: being billed after cancellation and requesting an itemized receipt, with no missing or incorrect information.
